description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly triangular profile and a prominent, dimpled surface texture on one side. The dimples are rounded and evenly distributed, giving the stone a distinctive, almost pitted appearance. The coloration is predominantly gray with subtle variations in tone, suggesting natural weathering. One side of the stone is smoother and less textured, while the opposite side features the most pronounced dimpling. There are some dark markings or numbers visible on the upper surface, possibly for cataloging or identification. The stone appears dense and solid, typical of materials used as urban obstacles or landscape features.
description: A gray, irregular stone with a distinctive dimpled surface on one side and subtle weathering marks. It is dense, solid, and marked with cataloging numbers.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from an angled perspective, highlighting its triangular shape and the start of the dimpled surface, with cataloging numbers visible.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the elongated profile and the full extent of the dimpled texture, with the cataloging numbers clearly visible along the upper edge.
description_view_2: The stone is shown from the smoother, less textured side, with the dimpled pattern receding and the overall form appearing more rounded.
description_view_3: A top-down view reveals the full dimpled pattern, showing the regular, rounded indentations covering most of the visible surface.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside verge, or landscaped public space
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Regular, rounded dimples cover one main surface, creating a pitted, tactile pattern that is visually striking and consistent.
attribute_weathering: The stone shows moderate weathering with a rough, matte finish and some surface smoothing, indicating exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
